title: Churn risk increased, find why
summary: Leadership sees retained revenue dropping in early cohorts and needs an analyst to isolate the churn driver, validate the signal with SQL, and recommend a focused retention action.
audience: Product leadership, lifecycle operations, and customer success
deliverable: Decision-ready churn diagnostic memo with query-backed evidence

dataset.name: cohort_accounts
dataset.description: Local mock churn dataset representing account cohorts, onboarding completion, retained revenue, and churn risk.

questions: Which onboarding cohort explains most of the retained-revenue decline?
Is the churn signal driven by onboarding completion, segment mix, or region?
What action should lifecycle leadership take first?
successCriteria: Use SQL to compare retained revenue and churn risk across cohort bands.
Identify the largest churn exposure by a useful business dimension.
Generate an insight that separates evidence from interpretation.
Submit a recommendation tied to a measurable operating action.

hints.text: Start by grouping cohort_accounts by onboarding_band, segment, or region and compare retained_revenue with churn_risk_score.
Run the query after the business dimension is clear. The strongest answer should reveal the highest-risk cohort, not just a broad average.
A strong insight names the cohort, connects retained-revenue loss to churn risk, and explains why the issue is actionable.
Tie the recommendation to onboarding intervention for the diagnosed cohort, not to a generic retention program.
